en.extract:
The yellow-bellied fantail (Chelidorhynx hypoxanthus), also known as the yellow-bellied fairy-fantail, is found in the Indian subcontinent, the Himalayas, and portions of Southeast Asia including Thailand, Vietnam, and Myanmar. It is an altitudinal migrant.


Description
It is about 8 cm (3.1 in) in size.  It is yellow below and has a black eye-stripe, white wing-bar and broad black tail tipped white.


Classification
It used to be placed in the family of the fantails (Rhipiduridae), but DNA analysis has shown it to be a close relative of the fairy flycatcher and it has therefore been transferred to the Stenostiridae, in the revalidated monotypic genus Chelidorhynx.


Social Behavior
It often gathers in mixed-species flocks, especially during winter, with leaf-warblers, tits, and black-faced warblers.


References


External links
Image at ADW

The '''yellow-bellied fantail''' ('''''Chelidorhynx hypoxanthus'''''), also known as the '''yellow-bellied fairy-fantail''', is found in the [[Indian subcontinent]], the [[Himalayas]], and portions of [[Southeast Asia]] including [[Thailand]], [[Vietnam]], and [[Myanmar]].<ref name=HBW>{{cite web | url = http://www.hbw.com/species/yellow-bellied-fantail-rhipidura-hypoxantha | title = Handbook of the Birds of the World Alive | doi = 10.2173/bow.yebfan1.01 | s2cid = 216411078 | access-date = 27 November 2015 }}</ref> It is an altitudinal migrant.<ref name=":0">{{cite taxon |BOW |author=Boles, W.|year= 2020|title= Yellow-bellied Fairy-Fantail (''Chelidorhynx hypoxanthus'')|version= 1.0|editor=J. del Hoyo|editor2= A. Elliott|editor3= J. Sargatal|editor4= D. A. Christie|editor5= E. de Juana|doi=10.2173/bow.yebfan1.01}}</ref>

== Description ==
It is about {{cvt|8|cm}} in size.  It is yellow below and has a black eye-stripe, white wing-bar and broad black tail tipped white.

== Classification ==
It used to be placed in the family of the [[fantail]]s ([[Rhipiduridae]]), but [[DNA]] analysis has shown it to be a close relative of the [[fairy flycatcher]] and it has therefore been transferred to the [[Stenostiridae]],<ref>{{BioRef |IOC |family=Stenostiridae|version= 15.1|date=2013-04-15}}</ref> in the revalidated [[monotypic]] [[genus]] '''''Chelidorhynx'''''.<ref>{{Cite journal | doi = 10.1016/j.ympev.2009.06.015| title = A new Indo-Malayan member of the Stenostiridae (Aves: Passeriformes) revealed by multilocus sequence data: Biogeographical implications for a morphologically diverse clade of flycatchers| journal = Molecular Phylogenetics and Evolution| volume = 53| issue = 2| pages = 384–93| year = 2009| last1 = Fuchs | first1 = J. R. M. | last2 = Pasquet | first2 = E. | last3 = Couloux | first3 = A. | last4 = Fjeldså | first4 = J. | last5 = Bowie | first5 = R. C. K. | pmid=19576994}}</ref>

== Social Behavior ==
It often gathers in mixed-species flocks, especially during winter, with [[Leaf warbler|leaf-warblers]], [[Tit (bird)|tits]], and [[Black-faced warbler|black-faced warblers]].<ref name=":0" />

[[File:Yellow-bellied fantail.jpg|thumb|left|Yellow-bellied fantail at Zuluk, Sikkim]]
